connectorUiConfig.title: Akamai Guardicore
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The Akamai Guardicore connector uses the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF) to import Agents, Assets, Applications, and Policy Rules from a Guardicore Centra instance into Microsoft Sentinel via Data Collection Rule (DCR) ingestion. The connector polls every 10 minutes via Sentinel's CCF runtime; no Azure Function App is deployed.

dataTypes.name: GuardicoreApplications_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
GuardicoreApplications_CL
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: GuardicoreAssets_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
GuardicoreAssets_CL
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: GuardicoreAgents_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
GuardicoreAgents_CL
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: GuardicorePolicyRules_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
GuardicorePolicyRules_CL
            | summarize Time = max(TimeGenerated)
            | where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and write permissions on the workspace are required.

instructionSteps.title: Connect Akamai Guardicore to Microsoft Sentinel
instructionSteps.description:
**Prerequisites**

1. You must have a reachable Akamai Guardicore Centra management instance with API access enabled.
2. Create (or obtain) an API user in Guardicore Centra with the **Read-only** role. This account must be authorized to call `/api/v3.0/authenticate`, `/api/v3.0/agents`, `/api/v3.0/assets`, `/api/v3.0/workflow/projects`, and `/api/v3.0/visibility/policy/rules`.
3. Have the Guardicore management URL (for example `https://<tenant>.cloud.guardicore.com`) and the service-account username and password ready.
4. The connector polls every 10 minutes via Sentinel's CCF runtime. No Azure Function App is deployed.

Provide your Guardicore Centra service-account details and click **Connect** to start polling.

dataFlows.transformKql: source | project-rename id = ['_id'] | extend TimeGenerated = now(), sampling_timestamp = toint(datetime_diff("second", bin(now(), 5m), datetime(1970-01-01))) | extend is_agent_enforcing = iff(tostring(parse_json(tostring(configuration)).enforcementagent.agent_operating_mode) == "Enforcing", true, false)
source | project-rename id = ['_id'] | extend TimeGenerated = now(), sampling_timestamp = toint(datetime_diff("second", bin(now(), 5m), datetime(1970-01-01)))
source | project-rename id = ['_id'] | extend TimeGenerated = now(), sampling_timestamp = toint(datetime_diff("second", bin(now(), 5m), datetime(1970-01-01)))
source | extend TimeGenerated = now(), sampling_timestamp = toint(datetime_diff("second", bin(now(), 5m), datetime(1970-01-01)))

metadata.description: Analyze Guardicore security incidents and their associated network connections. This dashboard provides detailed analysis of connection patterns, traffic analysis, internet traffic direction, and port distribution for selected incidents.

properties.serializedData:
{"version":"Notebook/1.0","items":[{"type":1,"content":{"json":"# Guardicore Incident Analysis Dashboard

This dashboard provides detailed analysis of security incidents and their associated network connections. Select an incident to view connection patterns and traffic analysis."},"name":"dashboard-intro","styleSettings":{"margin":"0 0 20px 0"}},{"type":9,"content":{"version":"KqlParameterItem/1.0","parameters":[{"id":"incident-selector","version":"KqlParameterItem/1.0","name":"SelectedIncident","label":"Select Incident","type":2,"isRequired":true,"query":"GuardicoreProcessedIncidents_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| distinct incidentTitle
| order by incidentTitle asc","typeSettings":{"resourceTypeFilter":{"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces":true},"showDefault":false},"timeContext":{"durationMs":86400000},"queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","value":""},{"id":"row-count-selector","version":"KqlParameterItem/1.0","name":"RowCount","label":"Number of connections to show (15 minutes related to incident)","type":2,"isRequired":true,"typeSettings":{"showDefault":false},"jsonData":"[
  { "value": 25, "label": "25" },
  { "value": 50, "label": "50" },
  { "value": 100, "label": "100" },
  { "value": 250, "label": "250" },
  { "value": 500, "label": "500" },
  { "value": 1000, "label": "1,000" },
  { "value": 10000, "label": "10,000" }
]","value":"1000"}],"style":"formVertical","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces"},"customWidth":"50","name":"incident-parameters"},{"type":12,"content":{"version":"NotebookGroup/1.0","groupType":"editable","title":"Connection Analysis","items":[{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"// Get slot identifiers for the selected incident
let incident_slots = GuardicoreProcessedIncidents_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where incidentTitle == '{SelectedIncident}'
| distinct slot_id;

// Get connections for those slot identifiers and count distinct connections by destination port
GuardicoreEnrichingConnections_CL
| where slot_identifier in (incident_slots)
| distinct id, destination_port, source_ip, destination_ip, slot_identifier
| where destination_port > 0
| summarize DistinctConnections = count() by tostring(toint(destination_port))
| top 10 by DistinctConnections desc
| extend Port = strcat(destination_port, " ", case(
  toint(destination_port) == 80, "(HTTP)",
  toint(destination_port) == 443, "(HTTPS)",
  toint(destination_port) == 22, "(SSH)",
  toint(destination_port) == 3389, "(RDP)",
  toint(destination_port) == 21, "(FTP)",
  toint(destination_port) == 25, "(SMTP)",
  toint(destination_port) == 53, "(DNS)",
  toint(destination_port) == 445, "(SMB)",
  toint(destination_port) == 3306, "(MySQL)",
  toint(destination_port) == 1433, "(MSSQL)",
  toint(destination_port) == 8086, "(InfluxDB)",
  toint(destination_port) == 8442, "(Custom)",
  ""))
| project Port, Count = DistinctConnections","size":0,"title":"Top Destination Ports","noDataMessage":"No connection data found for selected incident","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"barchart","chartSettings":{"xAxis":"Port","yAxis":["Count"],"seriesLabelSettings":[{"seriesName":"Count","color":"#0078D4"}],"ySettings":{"numberFormatSettings":{"unit":0,"options":{"style":"decimal","useGrouping":true}}}}},"customWidth":"50","name":"port-distribution-chart","styleSettings":{"maxWidth":"50%"}},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"// Get slot identifiers for the selected incident
let incident_slots = GuardicoreProcessedIncidents_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where incidentTitle == '{SelectedIncident}'
| distinct slot_id;

// Get connections for those slot identifiers and analyze internet traffic
GuardicoreEnrichingConnections_CL
| where slot_identifier in (incident_slots)
| distinct id, source_node_type, destination_node_type, count
| extend Direction = case(
    source_node_type == "internet", "From Internet",
    destination_node_type == "internet", "To Internet",
    "Internal"
)
| where Direction != "Internal"
| summarize ConnectionCount = sum(toint(count)) by Direction
| order by ConnectionCount desc","size":0,"title":"Internet Traffic Connections","noDataMessage":"No internet traffic found for selected incident","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"piechart","chartSettings":{"seriesLabelSettings":[{"seriesName":"From Internet","color":"orange"},{"seriesName":"To Internet","color":"blue"}]}},"customWidth":"50","name":"internet-traffic-chart","styleSettings":{"maxWidth":"50%"}},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"// Get slot identifiers for the selected incident
let incident_slots = GuardicoreProcessedIncidents_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where incidentTitle == '{SelectedIncident}'
| distinct slot_id;

// Get detailed connection information for internet traffic only
GuardicoreEnrichingConnections_CL
| where slot_identifier in (incident_slots)
| extend Direction = case(
    source_node_type == "internet", "From Internet",
    destination_node_type == "internet", "To Internet",
    "Internal"
)
| where Direction != "Internal"  // Only show internet traffic
| extend ConnectionTime = datetime_add('millisecond', tolong(slot_start_time), datetime(1970-01-01))
| project 
    ConnectionTime,
    SourceIP = source_ip,
    DestinationIP = destination_ip,
    DestinationPort = destination_port,
    Protocol = ip_protocol,
    ConnectionType = connection_type,
    ConnectionCount = count,
    Direction,
    PolicyVerdict = policy_verdict
| order by ConnectionTime desc
| limit toint({RowCount})","size":0,"title":"Internet Traffic Connections ({RowCount} entries)","noDataMessage":"No internet traffic found for selected incident","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"table","gridSettings":{"formatters":[{"columnMatch":"Direction","formatter":18,"formatOptions":{"thresholdsOptions":"colors","thresholdsGrid":[{"operator":"==","thresholdValue":"From Internet","representation":"redBright","text":"{0}{1}"},{"operator":"==","thresholdValue":"To Internet","representation":"orange","text":"{0}{1}"},{"operator":"Default","representation":"gray","text":"{0}{1}"}]}},{"columnMatch":"PolicyVerdict","formatter":18,"formatOptions":{"thresholdsOptions":"colors","thresholdsGrid":[{"operator":"==","thresholdValue":"allowed","representation":"green","text":"{0}{1}"},{"operator":"==","thresholdValue":"blocked","representation":"red","text":"{0}{1}"},{"operator":"Default","representation":"gray","text":"{0}{1}"}]}}],"rowLimit":10000,"filter":true,"sortBy":[{"itemKey":"DestinationPort","sortOrder":2}]},"sortBy":[{"itemKey":"DestinationPort","sortOrder":2}]},"name":"detailed-connections-table"}]},"conditionalVisibility":{"parameterName":"SelectedIncident","comparison":"isNotEqualTo"},"name":"connection-analysis-container"}],"fromTemplateId":"sentinel-UserWorkbook","$schema":"https://github.com/Microsoft/Application-Insights-Workbooks/blob/master/schema/workbook.json"}

properties.description: @{workbookKey=GuardicoreIncidentWorkbook; logoFileName=akamai-guardicore.svg; description=Analyze Guardicore security incidents and their associated network connections. This dashboard provides detailed analysis of connection patterns, traffic analysis, internet traffic direction, and port distribution for selected incidents.; dataTypesDependencies=System.Object[]; dataConnectorsDependencies=System.Object[]; previewImagesFileNames=System.Object[]; version=1.0.0; title=Guardicore Incident Analysis Dashboard; templateRelativePath=GuardicoreIndicent.json; subtitle=; provider=Akamai Guardicore}.description

metadata.description: Gain insights into Guardicore workload protection coverage, policy enforcement effectiveness, and security posture. This workbook provides visibility into protected workloads, policy rules by action type, blocking rulesets, and application status trends over time.

properties.serializedData:
{"version":"Notebook/1.0","items":[{"type":12,"content":{"version":"NotebookGroup/1.0","groupType":"editable","title":"Policy Enforcement Effectiveness","items":[{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"GuardicorePolicyRules_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by id
| where action contains "block"
| extend RulesetName = case(
    isempty(ruleset_name) or ruleset_name == "", "default",
    ruleset_name
)
| summarize TotalHitCount = sum(hit_count) by RulesetName
| top 10 by TotalHitCount desc
| project RulesetName, BlockingHits = TotalHitCount","size":0,"title":"Top Blocking Rulesets","noDataMessage":"No blocked traffic found","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"table"},"customWidth":"50","name":"top-blocking-rulesets","styleSettings":{"maxWidth":"50%"}},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"let startDate = startofday(ago(7d));
let endDate = startofday(now());
let all_days = range day from startDate to endDate step 1d;
// Get the latest record per application id per day
let dailyLatest = GuardicoreApplications_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= startDate
| extend day = startofday(TimeGenerated)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by id, day;
let daily_counts = dailyLatest
| extend Status = case(
    tolower(state) contains "block", "Block",
    tolower(state) contains "alert", "Alert",
    "Other"
)
| where Status in ("Block", "Alert")
| summarize
    Block = dcountif(project_name, Status == "Block"),
    Alert = dcountif(project_name, Status == "Alert")
    by day;
all_days
| join kind=leftouter (daily_counts) on day
| extend Block = coalesce(Block, 0),
         Alert = coalesce(Alert, 0)
| project day = format_datetime(day, "yyyy-MM-dd"), Block, Alert
| sort by day asc","size":0,"title":"Applications in Block vs. Alert Status","noDataMessage":"No applications with block/alert status found","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"unstackedbar","tileSettings":{"showBorder":false,"titleContent":{"columnMatch":"day","formatter":1},"leftContent":{"columnMatch":"Block","formatter":12,"formatOptions":{"palette":"auto"},"numberFormat":{"unit":17,"options":{"maximumSignificantDigits":3,"maximumFractionDigits":2}}}},"graphSettings":{"type":0,"topContent":{"columnMatch":"day","formatter":1},"centerContent":{"columnMatch":"Block","formatter":1,"numberFormat":{"unit":17,"options":{"maximumSignificantDigits":3,"maximumFractionDigits":2}}}},"chartSettings":{"xAxis":"day","seriesLabelSettings":[{"seriesName":"Block","color":"red"},{"seriesName":"Alert","color":"orange"}]}},"customWidth":"50","name":"applications-block-alert-status","styleSettings":{"maxWidth":"50%"}},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"GuardicorePolicyRules_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by id
| summarize count = count() by action
| extend actionFormatted = replace("_", " ", tolower(action))
| extend actionFormatted = strcat(toupper(substring(actionFormatted, 0, 1)), substring(actionFormatted, 1))
| project Action = actionFormatted, Count = count
| order by Count desc","size":0,"title":"Policy Rules by Action Type","noDataMessage":"No policy rules found","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"piechart","chartSettings":{"seriesLabelSettings":[{"seriesName":"Allow","color":"green"},{"seriesName":"Alert","color":"orange"},{"seriesName":"Block","color":"red"},{"seriesName":"Other","color":"gray"}]}},"name":"policy-rules-by-action"}]},"name":"policy-enforcement-container"},{"type":12,"content":{"version":"NotebookGroup/1.0","groupType":"editable","title":"🛡️ Workload Protection Coverage Dashboard","items":[{"type":1,"content":{"json":"This dashboard provides insights into workload protection status across your environment, including total workloads, protected workloads, and coverage trends over the last 7 days."},"name":"workload-protection-intro","styleSettings":{"margin":"0 0 20px 0"}},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"let startDate = startofday(ago(7d));
let endDate = startofday(now());
let all_days = range day from startDate to endDate step 1d;
// Get the latest record per agent id per day
let dailyLatest = GuardicoreAgents_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= startDate
| extend day = startofday(TimeGenerated)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by id, day;
let daily_counts = dailyLatest
| summarize
    total_agents = count(),
    protected_agents = countif(tostring(health.enforcement_agent.status) == "RUNNING" and is_agent_enforcing == true)
    by day;
all_days
| join kind=leftouter (daily_counts) on day
| extend total_agents = coalesce(total_agents, 0),
         protected_agents = coalesce(protected_agents, 0)
| project day = format_datetime(day, "yyyy-MM-dd"), total_agents, protected_agents
| sort by day asc
","size":1,"title":"📈 Protection Coverage Trend (7 Days)","noDataMessage":"No policy rules found in the specified time range","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"unstackedbar","chartSettings":{"xAxis":"day","yAxis":["total_agents","protected_agents"],"seriesLabelSettings":[{"seriesName":"total_agents","label":"Total Agents","color":"#0078D4"},{"seriesName":"protected_agents","label":"Protected Agents","color":"#107C10"}],"ySettings":{"numberFormatSettings":{"unit":0,"options":{"style":"decimal","useGrouping":true}},"min":0}}},"customWidth":"60","name":"active-rules-trend","styleSettings":{"maxWidth":"60%"}},{"type":3,"content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"let latestAgents = GuardicoreAgents_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by id;
let agentCount = toscalar(latestAgents | summarize count());
let protectedCount = toscalar(
    latestAgents
    | where tostring(health.enforcement_agent.status) == "RUNNING"
    | where is_agent_enforcing == true
    | summarize count()
);
print Category = "Total Workloads", Count = agentCount
| union (print Category = "Protected Workloads", Count = protectedCount)","size":1,"title":"🔢 Total vs Protected Workloads (Last 24h)","noDataMessage":"No workload data found","queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","visualization":"piechart","chartSettings":{"xAxis":"Category","yAxis":["Count"],"seriesLabelSettings":[{"seriesName":"Count","color":"blue"}]}},"customWidth":"40","name":"workloads-protected-chart","styleSettings":{"maxWidth":"40%"}}]},"name":"workload-protection-container"}],"fromTemplateId":"sentinel-UserWorkbook","$schema":"https://github.com/Microsoft/Application-Insights-Workbooks/blob/master/schema/workbook.json"}

properties.description: @{workbookKey=GuardicoreInfoWorkbook; logoFileName=akamai-guardicore.svg; description=Gain insights into Guardicore workload protection coverage, policy enforcement effectiveness, and security posture. This workbook provides visibility into protected workloads, policy rules by action type, blocking rulesets, and application status trends over time.; dataTypesDependencies=System.Object[]; dataConnectorsDependencies=System.Object[]; previewImagesFileNames=System.Object[]; version=1.0.0; title=Guardicore Workload Protection Dashboard; templateRelativePath=GuardicoreInfo.json; subtitle=; provider=Akamai Guardicore}.description

metadata.description: Name of the Microsoft Sentinel-enabled Log Analytics workspace that will receive enrichment data.
Resource group of the Sentinel workspace. Defaults to the deployment resource group.
Guardicore Centra management base URL, https only, no trailing slash. Example: https://yourcompany.cloud.guardicore.com
Guardicore service-account username (Read-only role is sufficient).
Name of the trigger Logic App. Default matches the legacy v1 playbook name so existing automation rules continue to match.
Name of the recurrence-triggered runner Logic App.
Optional. Globally unique 3-24 character lowercase alphanumeric storage account name. If left blank, a unique name is auto-generated.
Optional. Resource ID of an existing Data Collection Endpoint in the same region as the workspace. If left blank, a new DCE is created in this resource group.

resources.comments: 1 - Storage Account hosting the work-queue Azure Table.
resources.name: [[variables('actualStorageAccountName')]

resources.comments: 2 - Default tableServices on the storage account.
resources.name: [[concat(variables('actualStorageAccountName'), '/default')]

resources.comments: 4 - Optional DCE; created only if no existing DCE was supplied.
resources.name: [[variables('newDceName')]

resources.comments: 5 - Data Collection Rule routing the two enrichment streams to the workspace.
resources.name: [[variables('dcrName')]

dataFlows.transformKql: source | extend TimeGenerated = now(), sampling_timestamp = toint(datetime_diff("second", now(), datetime(1970-01-01)))

resources.comments: 7 - azuresentinel managed-API connection (used by trigger Logic App).
resources.name: [[variables('azureSentinelConnectionName')]

resources.comments: 8 - azuretables managed-API connection (shared between trigger and runner).
resources.name: [[variables('azureTablesConnectionName')]

resources.comments: 9 - Trigger Logic App: Sentinel-incident-creation triggered, fans out to 3 slots, writes manifest to GuardicoreProcessedIncidents_CL.
resources.name: [[parameters('PlaybookName')]

inputs.note: Insert failed (likely 409 Conflict); the existing PENDING/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED row is left untouched per dedup contract.

resources.comments: 10 - Runner Logic App: every 2 minutes, claim a due slot, fetch Guardicore connections, ingest to GuardicoreEnrichingConnections_CL.
resources.name: [[parameters('RunnerName')]

queries.$filter: @{concat('((status eq ''pending'' or status eq ''retry'') and slot_start le datetime''', outputs('Compute_eligibility_cutoff')['eligible_before'], ''') or (status eq ''in_progress'' and last_updated lt datetime''', outputs('Compute_eligibility_cutoff')['stale_before'], ''')')}

resources.comments: 11 - Role: trigger Logic App MI -> Monitoring Metrics Publisher on the DCR.
resources.name: [[guid(variables('dcrResourceId'), parameters('PlaybookName'), 'mmp')]

resources.comments: 12 - Role: runner Logic App MI -> Monitoring Metrics Publisher on the DCR.
resources.name: [[guid(variables('dcrResourceId'), parameters('RunnerName'), 'mmp')]

resources.comments: 13 - Role: trigger Logic App MI -> Storage Account Contributor (so MI can listKeys / read tables).
resources.name: [[guid(variables('storageAccountId'), parameters('PlaybookName'), 'sac')]

resources.comments: 14 - Role: runner Logic App MI -> Storage Account Contributor.
resources.name: [[guid(variables('storageAccountId'), parameters('RunnerName'), 'sac')]

resources.comments: 13 - Role: trigger Logic App MI -> Storage Account Contributor (so MI can listKeys / read tables).
resources.name: [[guid(variables('storageAccountId'), parameters('PlaybookName'), 'stdc')]

resources.comments: 14 - Role: runner Logic App MI -> Storage Account Contributor.
resources.name: [[guid(variables('storageAccountId'), parameters('RunnerName'), 'stdc')]

metadata.title: Akamai Guardicore Incident-Enrichment - one-click bootstrap
metadata.description: Deploys the entire Akamai Guardicore incident-enrichment stack in one shot: Storage Account + GuardicoreConnectionSlots Azure Table, Data Collection Endpoint (or reuses an existing one), Data Collection Rule, two custom log tables, two Logic Apps (trigger playbook + recurrence runner), and the role assignments the Logic Apps' managed identities need to write to the DCR and the storage account. After deployment, the only manual step is wiring the trigger playbook to a Sentinel automation rule.

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

- Review the solution Release Notes

- There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

Akamai Guardicore integration enables you to import connection, incident, and asset information into Microsoft Sentinel from an existing Guardicore instance.

Data Connectors: 1, Workbooks: 2, Playbooks: 1

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
